description: A postdoctoral researcher needs to measure the magnetic field alignment within the dust clouds of M78 (a famous reflection nebula in Orion) using linear polarimetry. Refer to the scientific observing request at ~/Documents/polarimetry_request.txt for specific coordinates and filter slot mappings. Slew the telescope to M78 and capture at least 3 LIGHT frames (120 seconds each) for each of the four polarization angles (0°, 45°, 90°, and 135°). Save the images for each angle to /home/ga/Images/polarimetry/M78/angle_XXX/ (where XXX is 000, 045, 090, or 135). Capture a wide-field context image (1.0 field of view, cool palette) to /home/ga/Images/polarimetry/M78/m78_context.png. Create a summary text file at ~/Documents/stokes_observation_log.txt listing the target, the 4 angles observed, and the number of frames successfully captured for each.
